zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud Davenport於1245年出生在英國爾達文波特時，她的父親Sir Roger Davenport20歲，母親Lady Mary Salemon17歲。她於1265年與Lord William Bulkeley在英國爾達文波特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子的父母。她去世於1313年之後在她的家鄉，埋葬在英國爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud Davenport于1245年出生在英国爾達文波特时，她的父亲Sir Roger Davenport20岁，母亲Lady Mary Salemon17岁。她于1265年与Lord William Bulkeley在英国爾達文波特结婚。他们至少是6个儿子的父母。她去世于1313年之后在她的家乡，埋葬在英国爾。

nameMeanings.name: Davenport
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): habitational name from a place in Cheshire named Davenport, from the Dane river (apparently named with a Celtic cognate of Middle Welsh dafnu ‘to drop, to trickle’) + Old English port ‘market town’.

Irish: in Tipperary, this is an English surname adopted by bearers of Munster Gaelic Ó Donndubhartaigh ‘descendant of Donndubhartach’, a personal name composed of the elements donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’ + dubh ‘black’ + artach ‘nobleman’.
History: John Davenport (died 1670) arrived in Boston, MA, in 1637. He came of an English Cheshire family associated with Capesthorne Hall, near Macclesfield.

variants.name: Devenport
meanings: English and Irish (Lancashire): variant of Davenport . The spelling Devonport is found in Ireland and it may have been this form which gave rise to Devenport.

variants.name: Dunworth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Eamon, Niall.
Irish: adopted in Ireland by bearers of Gaelic Ó Donndubhartaigh (see Davenport 2).
English: perhaps a variant of Dungworth, see Dunsworth .

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud Davenport was born in 1245, in Davenport, Cheshire, England, her father, Sir Roger Davenport, was 20 and her mother, Lady Mary Salemon, was 17. She married Lord William Bulkeley in 1265, in Davenport, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons. She died after 1313, in her hometown, and was buried in Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud Davenport est née en 1245, à Davenport, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Roger Davenport, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Lady Mary Salemon, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Lord William Bulkeley en 1265, à Davenport, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils. Elle est décédée après 1313, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à Cheshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud Davenport 1245 in Davenport, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Roger Davenport 20 und ihre Mutter Lady Mary Salemon 17. Sie hat 1265 in Davenport, Cheshire, England Lord William Bulkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne. Sie starb nach 1313 in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Cheshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Davenport è nata il nel 1245 nel Davenport, Cheshire, England, sua padre, Sir Roger Davenport, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Lady Mary Salemon, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Lord William Bulkeley nel 1265, nel Davenport, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli. Morì dopo il 1313, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolta a Cheshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud Davenportが1245年にイギリスチェシュアイアダベンプオルトで生まれた時，父Sir Roger Davenportは20歳，母Lady Mary Salemonは17歳であった。1265年にイギリスチェシュアイアダベンプオルトでLord William Bulkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子の親であった。彼女は1313年以後に故郷で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud Davenport(이)가 1245년 영국 체실르 대븐포트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Roger Davenport(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Mary Salemon(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 1265년 영국 체실르 대븐포트에서 Lord William Bulkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1313년 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 체실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud Davenport nasceu em 1245, em Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Roger Davenport, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Lady Mary Salemon, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Lord William Bulkeley em 1265, em Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos. Ela faleceu após 1313, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultada em Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud Davenport родилась в 1245 г. в местности давенпорт, чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Roger Davenport было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Mary Salemon, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord William Bulkeley в 1265 г. в местности давенпорт, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей. Она умерла после 1313 г. в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud Davenport nació en 1245, en Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Roger Davenport, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Lady Mary Salemon, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Lord William Bulkeley en 1265, en Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos. Murió después de 1313, en su pueblo, y fue sepultada en Cheshire, Inglaterra.
